item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote from me. I honestly don't get the whole "humans treat sapient AI as just a machine" trope, especially coming from the Foundation which already has other sapient AIs.

The fact that Thompson avoids any punishment for basically turning 5275 against the Foundation is also pretty weird to me.
Him mouthing off at all is also weird - he's a Senior Researcher, surely he had to have dealt with other sapient entities, right? Why would the Foundation tolerate that behavior? Not to mention, ticking off a Thaumiel skip is just dumb in general.

Researcher Jackson: […]unless it suddenly gives itself the idea that we've been lying to it over its status as an SCP.

This foreshadowing's pretty good, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This, this I can get behind! 5275 was fine until Thompson popped off, and this is what happens when you pick a fight with someone with better weapons than you have. In this case Thompson came prepared to put something in it place and brought a banana to a rocket launcher fight. :)

+1 and here's hoping Thompson gets more of a punishment than just resuming normal duties… because I think shitfuck just brought a shitstorm down on the place. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this concept, but my major gripe is that Thompson's aggressive and provoking attitude at the end didn't really feel… real? Like, as a senior researcher, surely antagonizing a hyper-intelligent AI is not a smart move- especially since this thing clearly used to be a Thaumiel skip and would have information regarding the fact that it is fairly free-willed. I just don't understand what the reasoning for his rant was in-universe- why was he so short-tempered with this thing?

I really liked this, the AI has real personality and the progression of utilization by the foundation makes a lot of sense… including the inevitable escalation to hostility

But I feel the final message from the AI is a bit much, I think you could pare it down to a minimal message that communicates hostile intent… to be honest, you could probably get rid of it altogether given the end of the conversation between the skip and the senior researchers, then the creation of the -1 instance

Either way, enjoyed the hell outta this

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this. There were some minor issues I had, but my major issue is big enough that it's the only one I'm going to mention: I refuse to believe that any single person in the foundation would decide to let a sapient AI program that they just simply found go ahead and work for them like that, let alone a unanimous decision. "Seems nice enough" doesn't really cut it, especially since it's a program. It could very easily lie and try to act convincingly.
I may have enjoyed it, but this colossal plothole is almost enough to downvote it, however I will simply abstain from voting at all.
In my humble opinion, you've done a masterful job of giving personality to this thing. So much so that the two dipshit researchers at the end seemed less human than the scip itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noticed a few typos:

+ show block
– hide block

it is to be immediately isolated from from Site-23's internal network

SCP-5275X:

Director Matthewss:

You think me as just a bunch of lines of code

That last one could be the AI itself missing a word, but it reads better with "think of me"

Re: the article itself, this line was kind of confusing. I've never heard the expression "a perfect hand".

a perfect hand on a silver platter.

I don't like how you gave Thompson the Idiot Ball. A Senior Researcher would know not to poke the SCP, especially if they'd worked with AI SCPs before.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
"You always get everything that you want. I can't explain, so I'll flaunt what you've got." -The Brobecks, If You Like It Or Not

If there's one thing you don't want to do, it's to lie to the sapient and very capable AI about the nature of its arrangement with you and then not expect it to get pissed off.

Thanks to JackalRelated, Modulum, Popsioak, and everybody else who looked over this for crit, and to whoever gave me these conprocs. Seriously, thank you for helping me to come up with this thing. I could not have done it without any of the aforementioned people, and I hope that you enjoyed this piece.
